title: Check Prime Number
description:
Determine if a given non-negative integer is a prime number.

A prime number is a number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.

visibleTestCases.explanation: 5 has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself, so it is a prime number.
4 is divisible by 2 (2 * 2 = 4), so it is not a prime number.

import sys
def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data: return
    n = int(input_data[0])
    print("true" if isPrime(n) else "false")
if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()

referenceSolution.completeCode:
# --- USER SOLUTION START ---
def isPrime(n):
    if n <= 1: return False
    if n <= 3: return True
    if n % 2 == 0 or n % 3 == 0: return False
    i = 5
    while i * i <= n:
        if n % i == 0 or n % (i + 2) == 0: return False
        i += 6
    return True
# --- USER SOLUTION END ---

import sys
def main():
    input_data = sys.stdin.read().split()
    if not input_data: return
    n = int(input_data[0])
    print("true" if isPrime(n) else "false")
if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
